note: BaseBox — Framer CMS Collection Schemas. Create each collection in Framer: CMS → Add Collection → name it exactly as shown. Then add fields in the order listed.

fields.name: Title
fields.example: How to Launch Your AI SaaS in 60 Minutes with Framer

fields.name: Excerpt
fields.note: Short summary shown on cards and meta description (160 chars max)
fields.example: Most founders waste weeks on landing page design. Here's how to go from zero to live in under an hour.

fields.name: Cover Image
fields.note: Recommended: 1200×630px, WEBP or JPG. Use Unsplash free images.

fields.name: Cover Image Alt
fields.note: Accessibility alt text for the cover image
fields.example: Dashboard analytics on a laptop screen

fields.name: Content
fields.note: Full post body. Use H2/H3 headings, paragraphs, and bullet lists.

fields.name: Category
fields.note: Single category label shown as a tag

fields.name: Author Avatar
fields.note: Author headshot. 80×80px, circular crop. Free to use from Unsplash or generated avatar.

fields.name: Featured
fields.note: Flag to pin posts to the top of the blog listing

fields.name: SEO Title
fields.note: Override the page <title> for SEO. Defaults to Title if empty.
fields.example: Launch AI SaaS in 60 Minutes | BaseBox Blog

sample_entries.Title: How to Launch Your AI SaaS in 60 Minutes with Framer
sample_entries.Excerpt: Most founders waste weeks agonizing over landing page design. Here's how to go from zero to live in under an hour.

sample_entries.Title: The Complete Guide to SaaS Pricing Strategy in 2026
sample_entries.Excerpt: Freemium, flat-rate, or usage-based? This guide breaks down which pricing model actually converts for early-stage AI products.

sample_entries.Title: Why Your Landing Page Isn't Converting (And How to Fix It)
sample_entries.Excerpt: Conversion experts analyzed 500 SaaS landing pages. These are the 7 mistakes killing your signups — and how BaseBox solves all of them.

collections.name: Changelog
collections.description: Product version history and release notes

fields.name: Title
fields.example: Dashboard UI overhaul + new chart types

fields.name: Tag
fields.note: Release type label: Major, Minor, Fix, or Hotfix

fields.name: Summary
fields.note: One-paragraph summary for the changelog listing
fields.example: Complete redesign of the Dashboard page with 4 new chart types, improved sidebar navigation, and mobile-responsive layout.

fields.name: Changes
fields.note: Bullet list of individual changes. Use H3 for groupings: New, Improved, Fixed.

fields.name: Breaking Changes
fields.note: Flag if this version contains breaking changes buyers should know about

sample_entries.Title: Framer Marketplace compliance + full accessibility pass
sample_entries.Summary: Full compliance update for Framer Marketplace submission. Adds 404 page, resolves all broken links, improves keyboard navigation and ARIA labels.

sample_entries.Title: 3-dropdown navigation + 7 new pages
sample_entries.Summary: Expanded the nav to a 3-dropdown system. Added AI Demo, CMS, Setup Guide, Changelog, Demo, License, and Privacy pages.

sample_entries.Title: Dark hero + gradient button system
sample_entries.Summary: Introduced dark mode hero section, unified gradient button system (blue→purple), and improved glass navbar visibility.

fields.name: Bio
fields.example: Previously at Stripe. Built 3 SaaS products before 30.

blog_detail.note: In Framer: Pages → Blog Post → set as CMS Page → bind to Blog Posts collection

framer_setup_steps: 2. Click CMS in the left panel
4. Name it exactly as above (e.g. 'Blog Posts')
5. Add each field with the correct type
6. Add at least 3 sample entries (required for Marketplace review)
7. On the Blog page, add a Collection List frame
9. Drop a BlogCard component inside the Collection List
10. Bind each BlogCard prop to the matching CMS field
11. Create a 'Blog Post' page, mark it as CMS Page
12. Bind the page to the Blog Posts collection
